scene: A snooker match at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield. One player led 8-3 and was expected by commentators to close out the match in two sessions; the trailing player instead fought back dramatically to level it and force a deciding frame. The comeback player has been playing, in the commentator's words, unreadable snooker this afternoon. The deciding frame is still in progress, with a chance on the table.

glossary.definition: The final frame of a match when both players are level; whoever wins it wins the match.
glossary.source_quote: just one chance in the deciding frame and this is a potential chance

glossary.definition: In major snooker matches, play is split into sessions of roughly 8 frames each, with breaks between them. At 8-3 the leader was expected to finish the job within the next session or two.

narratives.title: Deciding frame chance at the table
narratives.summary: A player has a chance on the table in the deciding frame. The commentator described it as a makeable opportunity the player must convert.

narratives.title: Remarkable comeback from 8-3 down
narratives.summary: One player trailed 8-3 and the match looked all but over; the trailing player has since played what the commentator calls unreadable snooker to claw all the way back to a deciding frame. The turnaround surprised even the commentators.

key_moments.what: Deciding frame, player at table with a chance
key_moments.why_it_matters: In snooker a deciding frame is sudden death -- whoever wins this frame wins the entire match.

key_moments.what: Commentator says the shot is among the easiest available and must be potted
key_moments.why_it_matters: Emphasis on 'must pot' signals a straightforward ball the player cannot afford to miss under this pressure.

key_moments.what: Commentator notes many expected one player to simply walk away with the match
key_moments.why_it_matters: Underscores how dominant the leader appeared before the comeback began.

key_moments.what: Score of 8-3 mentioned; commentator thought the leader could win it in two sessions
key_moments.why_it_matters: Puts the comeback in perspective: 8-3 in a long-format match is a commanding lead, making the comeback to a deciding frame all the more striking.

key_moments.what: Trailing player described as having scraped back and played unreadable snooker this afternoon
key_moments.why_it_matters: 'Unreadable' in snooker means the player's shot selection and safety play are hard to anticipate, making them very difficult to counter.
